title: Allocator field-level tracking undefined at task spawn — potential use-after-free
description: The spec says arena capabilities cannot cross task boundaries; spawning with Heap as alloc is required. But it does not define what happens when a struct containing arena-allocated fields is moved into a Heap task. If field-level allocators are not tracked, a struct with a nested arena-allocated slice can be moved into the task, the arena scope exits and frees memory, and the task holds a dangling pointer. This is a potential use-after-free in structured concurrency that is invisible to the type system. The spec must either formally define field-level allocator tracking rules, or prohibit passing structs with non-Heap fields across task boundaries.
